name: Saône-et-Loire (France)
description: A composite design: in the upper hoist a blue field strewn with yellow fleurs-de-lis within a red-and-white chequy border (Burgundy modern); in the upper fly bendy of yellow and blue within a red border (Burgundy ancient); across the lower part a yellow field with two blue wavy vertical stripes.
history: The flag joins the two historic arms of Burgundy above the two wavy stripes representing the rivers Saône and Loire.

features.role: two Burgundy arms over a wavy-striped base
two blue wavy pales for the rivers
